title: The Inevitability of Memory Cycles: A History of Booms and Busts
title_translation: 存储周期的必然性：繁荣与萧条的历史

paragraphs.source: Since its commercial introduction in the 1970s, DRAM has benefited from the two scaling laws that defined the semiconductor industry: Moore’s Law and Dennard scaling. The 1T1C DRAM cell, with one access transistor and one storage capacitor, scaled for decades. Shrinking transistors reduced cost per bit, while clever capacitor engineering preserved sufficient charge to maintain signal integrity.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Since its commercial introduction in the 1970s, DRAM has benefited from the two scaling laws that defined the semiconductor industry: Moore’s Law and Dennard scaling. The 1T1C DRAM cell, with one access transistor and one storage capacitor, scaled for decades. Shrinking transistors reduced cost per bit, while clever capacitor engineering preserved sufficient charge to maintain signal integrity.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Since its commercial introduction in the 1970s, DRAM has benefited from the two scaling laws that defined the semiconductor industry: Moore’s Law and Dennard scaling. The 1T1C DRAM cell, with one access transistor and one storage capacitor, scaled for decades. Shrinking transistors reduced cost per bit, while clever capacitor engineering preserved sufficient charge to maintain signal integrity.

pro.text: 自 20 世纪 70 年代投入商业化应用以来，DRAM 一直受益于定义半导体行业的两大缩放定律：摩尔定律与登纳德缩放定律。包含一个存取晶体管和一个存储电容的 1T1C DRAM 存储单元（1T1C DRAM cell），在过去几十年里持续微缩。晶体管微缩降低了每比特成本，而精妙的电容工程设计则保留了充足电荷，确保了信号完整性。

applied_terms: Hardcore tech terms must be rigorously accurate: '1T1C DRAM cell' (1T1C DRAM存储单元), 'access transistor' (存取晶体管), 'storage capacitor' (存储电容), 'Dennard scaling' (登纳德缩放定律).
Translate 'deflationary force' in an economic context (通缩力量/降价驱动力).

understanding.role_in_article: Provides historical and technical context to explain the structural shift in the DRAM industry from technology-driven deflation to capacity-driven cyclicality.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Fulfills the promise made at the end of the intro to provide 'background' before diving into current market dynamics.
understanding.relation_to_next: Establishes the physical limitations (slowing density gains) that directly cause the market behaviors and capital indiscipline discussed next.
key_points: DRAM historically scaled via Moore's Law and Dennard scaling using the 1T1C cell structure.
As density gains hit physical limits, the traditional deflationary force of technology-driven cost-per-bit reduction has slowed.
DRAM pricing is now dictated primarily by capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics.
translation_notes: Hardcore tech terms must be rigorously accurate: '1T1C DRAM cell' (1T1C DRAM存储单元), 'access transistor' (存取晶体管), 'storage capacitor' (存储电容), 'Dennard scaling' (登纳德缩放定律).
Translate 'deflationary force' in an economic context (通缩力量/降价驱动力).

issues.description: 译文将 'commoditized product' 译为 '高度商品化的产品'。根据翻译指南，半导体语境下的 'Commodity'（如 Commodity DRAM）应译为 '标准型' 或 '大宗'，以符合行业标准术语。
译文为 '电容已演变为极端的极高深宽比三维结构，深宽比逼近 100:1'。译者在此处超译了 '极高深宽比'，与后文的 '深宽比逼近 100:1' 产生了语义重复，且原文仅为 'extreme three-dimensional structures'。

paragraphs.source: For much of the industry’s history, DRAM density scaled faster than logic, doubling roughly every 18 months instead of 24 months and driving dramatic cost reductions. As a commoditized product, manufacturers needed to sustain cost-per-bit declines to stay competitive. Suppliers who couldn’t compete on cost fell into a downward spiral: low sales left them short on cash to finance next-generation nodes, which in turn left them further behind on cost-per-bit. Many DRAM producers fell victim and went into bankruptcy, resulting in consolidation to just a few major players today.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: For much of the industry’s history, DRAM density scaled faster than logic, doubling roughly every 18 months instead of 24 months and driving dramatic cost reductions. As a commoditized product, manufacturers needed to sustain cost-per-bit declines to stay competitive. Suppliers who couldn’t compete on cost fell into a downward spiral: low sales left them short on cash to finance next-generation nodes, which in turn left them further behind on cost-per-bit. Many DRAM producers fell victim and went into bankruptcy, resulting in consolidation to just a few major players today.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: For much of the industry’s history, DRAM density scaled faster than logic, doubling roughly every 18 months instead of 24 months and driving dramatic cost reductions. As a commoditized product, manufacturers needed to sustain cost-per-bit declines to stay competitive. Suppliers who couldn’t compete on cost fell into a downward spiral: low sales left them short on cash to finance next-generation nodes, which in turn left them further behind on cost-per-bit. Many DRAM producers fell victim and went into bankruptcy, resulting in consolidation to just a few major players today.

pro.text: 在行业发展的大部分时间里，DRAM 密度的微缩速度远超逻辑芯片，大约每 18 个月（而非 24 个月）翻一番，推动成本大幅下降。作为高度商品化的产品，制造商必须持续压低每比特成本，才能维持竞争力。无法在成本上竞争的供应商陷入恶性循环：销量低迷导致现金流枯竭，无力投资下一代节点；这又反过来导致其在每比特成本上进一步落后。众多 DRAM 生产商因此沦为牺牲品并走向破产，行业历经整合，最终演变为如今仅剩几家巨头鼎立的格局。

paragraphs.source: Yet DRAM scaling has slowed significantly over the past few decades, and density gains over time have shrunk. Over the past decade, DRAM density has increased by only ~2× in total, versus roughly ~100× per decade during the industry’s peak scaling era. Capacitors are now extreme three-dimensional structures with aspect ratios approaching 100:1, storing just tens of thousands of electrons. For comparison, a small static shock when you touch a metal doorknob might involve the transfer of billions of electrons. The static charge on just a speck of dust might be 10,000x what is stored in a modern DRAM cell.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Yet DRAM scaling has slowed significantly over the past few decades, and density gains over time have shrunk. Over the past decade, DRAM density has increased by only ~2× in total, versus roughly ~100× per decade during the industry’s peak scaling era. Capacitors are now extreme three-dimensional structures with aspect ratios approaching 100:1, storing just tens of thousands of electrons. For comparison, a small static shock when you touch a metal doorknob might involve the transfer of billions of electrons. The static charge on just a speck of dust might be 10,000x what is stored in a modern DRAM cell.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Yet DRAM scaling has slowed significantly over the past few decades, and density gains over time have shrunk. Over the past decade, DRAM density has increased by only ~2× in total, versus roughly ~100× per decade during the industry’s peak scaling era. Capacitors are now extreme three-dimensional structures with aspect ratios approaching 100:1, storing just tens of thousands of electrons. For comparison, a small static shock when you touch a metal doorknob might involve the transfer of billions of electrons. The static charge on just a speck of dust might be 10,000x what is stored in a modern DRAM cell.

pro.text: 然而，过去几十年间 DRAM 的微缩步伐已显著放缓，密度随时间提升的幅度也在不断萎缩。过去十年里，DRAM 密度总共仅提升了约 2 倍；而在行业微缩的巅峰时期，每十年的提升幅度高达约 100 倍。如今，电容已演变为极端的极高深宽比三维结构，深宽比逼近 100:1，内部仅存储数万个电子。相比之下，当你触碰金属门把手时产生的微弱静电冲击，就可能涉及数十亿个电子的转移。仅仅一粒灰尘上携带的静电电荷，就可能是现代 DRAM 存储单元所存电荷的 10000 倍。

paragraphs.source: Bitlines and sense amplifiers, once secondary concerns, are now dominant constraints. Every incremental shrink reduces signal margin, increases variability, and raises cost.

pro.text: 位线与读出放大器曾经只是次要问题，如今却成了核心瓶颈。每一次增量微缩都会压缩信号裕度、加剧变异性，并推高成本。

paragraphs.source: An easy way to understand the technical challenge in DRAM scaling is to think of a DRAM cell as a tiny bucket that holds electricity instead of water. Each bucket stores a bit of data by holding a small electrical charge. Over the years, engineers made these buckets smaller to fit more memory on a chip. At first this worked well. But today, those buckets are not just tall they are tall and narrow, each is like a tiny drinking straw standing upright. Because of the size each bucket now holds very very few electrons.

But today, those buckets are not just tall they are tall and narrow, each is like a tiny drinking straw standing upright. Because of the size each bucket now holds very very few electrons.

Each bucket stores a bit of data by holding a small electrical charge. Over the years, engineers made these buckets smaller to fit more memory on a chip. At first this worked well. But today, those buckets are not just tall they are tall and narrow, each is like a tiny drinking straw standing upright. Because of the size each bucket now holds very very few electrons.

pro.text: 要理解 DRAM 微缩面临的技术挑战，不妨将 DRAM 存储单元想象成一个装电而非装水的小桶。每个小桶通过留存微量电荷来存储一比特数据。多年来，工程师不断缩小这些小桶的体积，以便在单块芯片上塞入更多内存。这在初期成效显著。然而时至今日，这些小桶不仅变得极高，而且又高又窄，简直就像一根根直立的极细吸管。受制于物理尺寸，如今每个小桶所能容纳的电子数量已寥寥无几。

applied_terms: Hardcore tech terms must be rigorously accurate: '1T1C DRAM cell' (1T1C DRAM存储单元), 'access transistor' (存取晶体管), 'storage capacitor' (存储电容), 'Dennard scaling' (登纳德缩放定律).
Translate 'deflationary force' in an economic context (通缩力量/降价驱动力).

understanding.role_in_article: Provides historical and technical context to explain the structural shift in the DRAM industry from technology-driven deflation to capacity-driven cyclicality.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Fulfills the promise made at the end of the intro to provide 'background' before diving into current market dynamics.
understanding.relation_to_next: Establishes the physical limitations (slowing density gains) that directly cause the market behaviors and capital indiscipline discussed next.
key_points: DRAM historically scaled via Moore's Law and Dennard scaling using the 1T1C cell structure.
As density gains hit physical limits, the traditional deflationary force of technology-driven cost-per-bit reduction has slowed.
DRAM pricing is now dictated primarily by capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics.

issues.description: 译文将 'commoditized product' 译为 '高度商品化的产品'。根据翻译指南，半导体语境下的 'Commodity'（如 Commodity DRAM）应译为 '标准型' 或 '大宗'，以符合行业标准术语。
译文为 '电容已演变为极端的极高深宽比三维结构，深宽比逼近 100:1'。译者在此处超译了 '极高深宽比'，与后文的 '深宽比逼近 100:1' 产生了语义重复，且原文仅为 'extreme three-dimensional structures'。

paragraphs.source: This is a problem. When the system tries to read the data, it has to detect this very faint electrical signal and distinguish it from noise. The wires that connect these cells (the “bitline”) and the tiny sensors that read them (called sense amplifiers) are now the main bottleneck. The signal is so weak that even small variations in manufacturing or temperature can cause errors.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: This is a problem. When the system tries to read the data, it has to detect this very faint electrical signal and distinguish it from noise. The wires that connect these cells (the “bitline”) and the tiny sensors that read them (called sense amplifiers) are now the main bottleneck. The signal is so weak that even small variations in manufacturing or temperature can cause errors.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: This is a problem. When the system tries to read the data, it has to detect this very faint electrical signal and distinguish it from noise. The wires that connect these cells (the “bitline”) and the tiny sensors that read them (called sense amplifiers) are now the main bottleneck. The signal is so weak that even small variations in manufacturing or temperature can cause errors.

pro.text: 问题随之而来。当系统尝试读取数据时，必须捕捉这种极其微弱的电信号，并将其与背景噪声区分开来。连接这些存储单元的导线（即“位线”）以及负责读取信号的微型传感器（即“读出放大器”）如今已成为核心瓶颈。由于信号过于微弱，哪怕是制造工艺或温度出现极小波动，都可能引发数据错误。

paragraphs.source: Together, these constraints explain why DRAM density has stagnated and why DRAM scaling has slowed down significantly over the years. The collapse of DRAM scaling has far-reaching consequences across cost, architecture, and industry structure.

pro.text: 种种物理限制叠加，解释了为何 DRAM 密度陷入停滞，以及为何其微缩步伐在过去数年间大幅放缓。DRAM 微缩进程的崩塌，正对成本控制、底层架构乃至整个行业的竞争格局产生深远影响。

paragraphs.source: As density gains slow, cost per bit reductions have slowed down. DRAM pricing is now more dependent on capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics rather than technology-driven cost reductions which have been a powerful deflationary force.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: As density gains slow, cost per bit reductions have slowed down. DRAM pricing is now more dependent on capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics rather than technology-driven cost reductions which have been a powerful deflationary force.

pro.text: 随着密度增长放缓，每比特成本的下降速度也随之放缓。过去，技术驱动的成本下降曾是一股强大的通缩力量；而如今，DRAM 的定价逻辑已发生根本转变，愈发取决于新增产能与周期性的供需博弈。
